title: QUIC RFC 9000 Path Validation Response and Retry Verification
verification_method: Requirement-home execution, deterministic state-machine fuzz topoffs, existing PATH_CHALLENGE/PATH_RESPONSE frame-codec fuzz coverage, BenchmarkDotNet Dry validation, and SpecTrace render/check for the bounded path-validation response and retry slice.
scope: This verification covers the supported runtime path-validation floor: starting candidate-path validation on apparent migration, retransmitting PATH_CHALLENGE while validation remains needed, clearing retry after validation or abandonment, sending one matching PATH_RESPONSE for a received PATH_CHALLENGE, and closing without promotion on mismatched PATH_RESPONSE payloads.
expected_result: Focused tests prove positive and negative path-validation retry behavior, deterministic fuzz topoffs cover representative retry payload changes and one-shot PATH_RESPONSE payload echoing, existing frame-codec fuzz coverage remains linked for PATH_CHALLENGE and PATH_RESPONSE parse/format boundaries, the new BenchmarkDotNet Dry suite executes, and the proof stays limited to the bounded runtime floor.
procedure: Run the focused RFC 9000 requirement-home tests for S9P3P3, S13P3-0026 through S13P3-0028, S19P17-0006, and S19P18-0003.
Run the existing PATH_CHALLENGE and PATH_RESPONSE frame-codec fuzz tests.
Run the `QuicPathValidationBenchmarks` BenchmarkDotNet suite with the Dry job.
Render and check the touched SpecTrace artifacts.
Confirm that no broader migration, ECN, public API, or sender scheduler claim is introduced.
preconditions: The active runtime can create candidate paths and path-validation timer state.
The application-data packet opener can process protected PATH_CHALLENGE and PATH_RESPONSE frames.
The PATH_CHALLENGE and PATH_RESPONSE frame codec remains strict about eight-byte payloads.

supplemental_sections.heading: Fuzz And Benchmark Scope
supplemental_sections.content: Deterministic requirement-home fuzz topoffs cover representative path identities, retry timer generations, challenge-payload changes, and one-shot PATH_RESPONSE echo behavior. Existing frame-codec fuzz coverage remains the untrusted parse/format proof for PATH_CHALLENGE and PATH_RESPONSE payload boundaries. The permanent benchmark suite covers helper and transition harness execution only; Dry validation is not treated as a stable performance baseline.

status_summary: Passed locally on 2026-04-25. Focused requirement-home tests for the bounded path-validation response and retry slice passed 21/21, the PATH_CHALLENGE/PATH_RESPONSE frame-codec guard passed 5/5, and the new QuicPathValidationBenchmarks Dry suite executed all 3 cases. Dry benchmark results are harness evidence only and are not treated as stable performance baselines.
